projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: Consistent evening energy spike: avg peak 4.95 kWh/hr typically around 20:00 PDT across 4 days. Likely AC/HVAC cooling cycle. At $0.35/kWh this peak window costs ~$10.39/evening.
Energy consumption trending up 35%: recent avg 59.6 kWh/day vs earlier 44.0 kWh/day. This covers 4 days of data.
[dog_run] The camera stream experienced a momentary failure where no frames were received.

There is a high concentration of repeated, unclassified audio events (doorbell, backyard, dog_run, front_yard) occurring in rapid succession between 05:30 AM and 05:36 AM. This multi-zone activity suggests a repeated stimulus or disturbance, but the cameras show no corresponding visual evidence of people, animals, or vehicles, indicating the source is either highly transient, outside the camera's view, or a persistent environmental noise that requires investigation.
[front_yard] The camera stream experienced a brief interruption where no frames were received.
[dog_run] The camera feed experienced a momentary loss of frames.

There is a pattern of multiple, unclassified audio events detected at the front door (04:19, 04:23, 04:26, 04:32) occurring repeatedly during the very early morning hours. Given the time and the general quietness of the house, these repeated, unclassified sounds are unusual and suggest potential activity or noise source outside that warrants checking, even if the cameras are currently experiencing technical issues.

There are multiple unclassified audio events detected at the front door, front yard, and backyard (3:18, 3:21, 3:22, 3:32). Since the camera system has experienced multiple high-severity failures, these repeated, unexplained sounds suggest persistent external activity (e.g., a visitor or animal) that the visual sensors failed to capture, warranting a check of the perimeter.
[dog_run] The camera stream experienced a momentary failure.

The unclassified doorbell audio detected at 02:25 AM, occurring during a period of otherwise complete silence and no visible activity on any exterior camera, suggests a potential late-night visitor or disturbance that was not fully captured or identified by the system. This warrants a check of the front door area.
